creator: des@FreeBSD.org
text:
This is more of a support issue than a bug, so you should have posted it on stable@freebsd.org rather than here, but I'm happy to help.

To summarize: you installed lighttpd, then waited a bit, and tried pkg update again, which failed?

The passage of time is not irrelevant—the cached copy of the repo metadata expired, so `pkg` won't do anything that involves the repo without first updating its cache.

Before you do anything else, please revert your /etc/pkg/FreeBSD.conf to the stock version and remove any custom repositories:

# fetch -o /etc/pkg http://svn.freebsd.org/base/releng/11.0/etc/pkg/FreeBSD.conf
# find /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos -type f -delete

Next, try `pkg-static update -f`.  If this works, but `pkg update -f` doesn't, run `pkg-static install -fy pkg` and then try `pkg update -f` again.

If this did not fix the problem, i.e. `pkg-static` works but `pkg` doesn't, you have a library compatibility issue.  Note that by installing lighttpd, you also installed the ports version of OpenSSL (which provides RSA), so this may be related; it shouldn't make any difference since they have different shlib major numbers, but I don't want to rule anything out.  Please run `ldd /usr/local/sbin/pkg`, then `env LD_UTRACE=yes ktrace -tu pkg update -f ; kdump | grep loaded` and show me the output.
